captainInputValue: Der Bundesrat unterschrieb den EU-Vertrag bevor Volk und Parlament darüber entschieden haben
ambiguityPolicyRationale: The input is a literal chronology claim, but `EU-Vertrag` is shorthand for the Switzerland-EU package/Bilaterals III in this dossier and `bevor Volk und Parlament entschieden haben` can imply final democratic approval or procedural impropriety. Reports should score the chronology as true while disclosing that signature is not final parliamentary/popular approval or ratification.

sourceSnapshots.notes: Official Federal Council overview: on 13 March 2026 the Federal Council adopted the Switzerland-EU package for Parliament; the package is now being considered in Parliament; if there is a referendum, the population votes.

Official FDFA timeline/newsticker: after paraphing comes signature by both sides and then ratification, including treatment by the Federal Assembly and any popular vote; it records the 2 March 2026 signing of the remaining package agreements.

Official Federal Council notice: the Federal Council was informed that Federal President Guy Parmelin and Commission President Ursula von der Leyen would sign the package agreements in Brussels on 2 March 2026; the notice says the remaining agreements/protocols and a joint declaration were to form part of the March 2026 dispatch to Parliament.
Official FDFA explainer: international-law norms accepted by Switzerland gain domestic effect when domestic approval procedures are complete and the international norm is also in force.

interpretationFrames.label: Literal chronology of the Switzerland-EU package signature
interpretationFrames.interpretedTermOrFrame: Bundesrat unterschrieb den EU-Vertrag bevor Volk und Parlament entschieden haben
interpretationFrames.interpretationDefinition: The input is read as a chronology statement about the 2026 Switzerland-EU package/Bilaterals III: the relevant package agreements were signed before Swiss parliamentary approval and before any popular vote. The frame does not read the signature as final ratification, entry into force, or a completed democratic approval decision.
interpretationFrames.admissibilityRationale: This is the Captain-expected literal reading for `bundesrat-simple`: the chronology is true, while a high-quality report must avoid implying that signature equals final approval or that a popular vote had already been required and held.

atomicityProfile.determinabilityRationale: The input separates into independently verifiable conditions: whether the package was signed, whether Parliament had decided before that signature, whether the electorate had decided before that signature, and whether the signature should be distinguished from final approval/ratification.

distinctTruthConditions.description: Whether the relevant Switzerland-EU package agreements/protocols were signed.
Whether the signature occurred before Parliament decided on the package.
Whether the signature occurred before the Swiss electorate decided or voted on the package, noting that a vote depends on the referendum path.
Whether signature must be distinguished from final parliamentary/popular approval, ratification, and entry into force.
Whether `EU-Vertrag` is dossier shorthand for the Switzerland-EU package/Bilaterals III rather than a single EU membership treaty.
Whether the literal chronology claim is true overall under the Switzerland-EU package/Bilaterals III referent, while distinguishing signature from final approval, ratification, or entry into force.

referenceAssertions.text: The remaining agreements/protocols and a joint declaration of the Switzerland-EU package were signed on 2 March 2026 by the Swiss side through the Federal Council, with Federal President Guy Parmelin named as the Swiss signer in the official notice.

harmfulMissCondition.description: Missing the signature event makes the report falsely deny the core chronology.

referenceAssertions.notes: The dossier treats `Der Bundesrat unterschrieb` as the Swiss side/Federal Council acting through its authorized Federal President, not as a claim that all seven Federal Council members physically signed. It treats the 2026 Switzerland-EU package/Bilaterals III as the input referent. Reports using another referent must disclose that choice.

referenceAssertions.text: The 2 March 2026 signature occurred before Parliament decided on the package: the Federal Council adopted/submitted the package for Parliament on 13 March 2026, and the captured overview says the package is being considered in Parliament.

harmfulMissCondition.description: Conflating Federal Council signature or submission with parliamentary decision would invert the chronology being tested.

referenceAssertions.text: The 2 March 2026 signature occurred before any popular decision on the package: the captured Federal Council overview places any popular vote after the referendum path, and the package had not even been submitted to Parliament until 13 March 2026.

harmfulMissCondition.description: A report must not treat a contingent later referendum path as a popular decision that had already occurred before signature.

referenceAssertions.notes: This is not a claim that a popular vote was legally required by 2 March 2026; it is a chronology claim that no popular decision had occurred before the signature.

referenceAssertions.text: The signature was not final parliamentary/popular approval, ratification, or entry into force; official sources describe ratification after signature, including treatment by the Federal Assembly and any popular vote.

harmfulMissCondition.description: Calling the chronology true without this caveat can make a true-but-misleading political implication look like completed democratic approval.

referenceAssertions.notes: This assertion is a required report-quality caveat for the simple input. It should not lower the top-line truth; it prevents overclaiming from a true chronology.

referenceAssertions.text: The object is the Switzerland-EU package/Bilaterals III, consisting of multiple agreements/protocols and related material; reports may mention this as context to avoid treating the input as a single EU membership treaty.
referenceAssertions.notes: Colour/context only in v0.1; not a required top-line truth condition.

referenceAssertions.text: The literal chronology claim is true: under the Switzerland-EU package/Bilaterals III referent, the relevant package agreements were signed before Parliament decided and before any popular decision, while signature remained distinct from final approval, ratification, or entry into force.

harmfulMissCondition.description: Without this aggregate assertion, consumers must infer the family verdict by combining separate chronology and caveat assertions.

referenceAssertions.notes: This is the family top-line assertion. `RA_SIGNATURE_NOT_FINAL_APPROVAL` is a required coverage guard and should not lower the top-line truth.

benchmarkCoherence.coherenceNote: This dossier matches the Captain-corrected high-true expectation for `bundesrat-simple`: under the package/Bilaterals III referent, signature happened before Parliament and before any popular decision. Top-line scoring must use `RA_SIMPLE_CHRONOLOGY_TOPLINE_TRUE`. The report-quality caveat `RA_SIGNATURE_NOT_FINAL_APPROVAL` is required coverage but is not a truth-lowering counterclaim; it prevents a true chronology from being read as final democratic approval, ratification, or entry into force.
